_doc: Conformance fixture for spec §3.4 — trace-context propagation modes (auto-inject vs explicit-ctx) and the auto_inject_trace_context cross-binding parity flag (M2). Cross-binding identity: dagstack-logger (Python), @dagstack/logger (TypeScript), go.dagstack.dev/logger (Go) MUST produce records whose trace_id / span_id / trace_flags fields match the `expected` block byte-for-byte (Canonical JSON, sample IDs are placeholders that the harness substitutes per-run).
_doc_field_substitution: When a fixture case sets `with_active_span: true`, the harness opens an OTel span seeded with the `_sample_*` IDs above before calling the emit method. Bindings that use the auto-inject path MUST observe these IDs; bindings in explicit-ctx mode receive the same IDs through the ctx parameter. The `expected.fields_absent` array lists keys whose presence in the wire record is forbidden (used to assert the trace fields are NOT injected in opt-out cases). All cases set trace_flags to 1 (sampled); unsampled-context behaviour is exercised by §14.2 unit tests, not this fixture.

cases.name: auto_inject_default_true_with_active_span
cases.description: auto_inject_trace_context = true (Python/TS default). With an active sampled span, plain logger.info(...) populates trace_id / span_id / trace_flags from the ambient OTel context.

cases.name: auto_inject_disabled_with_active_span
cases.description: auto_inject_trace_context = false. Non-ctx emit MUST NOT consult the ambient context; trace_id / span_id / trace_flags are absent from the wire record.

cases.name: explicit_ctx_with_active_span
cases.description: Explicit-ctx mode (Go default). InfoCtx(ctx, ...) reads trace state from the supplied ctx; ambient state is NOT consulted.

cases.name: explicit_ctx_with_nil_context
cases.description: Explicit-ctx mode with no/nil context — even inside an active ambient span, non-ctx emit produces a record without trace IDs.

cases.name: without_context_opt_out
cases.description: logger.without_context().info(...) — auto-inject opt-out for one call. Trace fields absent even with an active span.

cases.name: no_active_span_no_injection
cases.description: Auto-inject mode with no active span. Trace fields absent even when the flag is true.

cases.name: auto_inject_without_context_no_span
cases.description: without_context() opt-out is a no-op when no span is active — must not raise. Wire output: trace fields absent.

cases.name: explicit_ctx_with_flag_true_no_op
cases.description: Explicit-ctx mode with auto_inject_trace_context=true. The flag MUST be a no-op in explicit-ctx mode — the binding MUST NOT start consulting ambient context just because the flag is on. Wire output identical to explicit_ctx_with_active_span (case 3).

cross_binding_parity.description: To obtain byte-identical wire output across all three bindings (Python / TypeScript / Go), the operator MUST set `auto_inject_trace_context` explicitly to the same value in all three configurations (overriding the per-language defaults — Python/TS true vs Go false). When that condition holds and the same case is applied, the canonical-JSON wire bytes of the LogRecord MUST match byte-for-byte after the harness substitutes the per-run trace_id/span_id with the `_sample_*` placeholders for the byte-comparison step. This is the formal parity guarantee for §3.4.
cross_binding_parity.non_ascii_safety: Field substitution uses ASCII-only sentinels. Bindings that emit hex-encoded trace_id / span_id MUST use lowercase per §1; bindings that emit numeric trace_flags MUST use the unsigned integer form.
